commands.name: collect
commands.description: Collects a diagnostic trace from a currently running process or launch a child process and trace it. Append -- to the collect command to instruct the tool to run a command and trace it immediately. When tracing a child process, the exit code of dotnet-trace shall be that of the traced process unless the trace process encounters an error.

options.name: --process-id
options.description: The process id to collect the trace.

options.name: --buffersize
options.description: Sets the size of the in-memory circular buffer in megabytes. Default 256 MB. [default: 256]

options.name: --output
options.description: The output path for the collected trace data. If not specified it defaults to '<appname>_<yyyyMMdd>_<HHmmss>.nettrace', e.g., 'myapp_20210315_111514.nettrace'. [default: default]

options.name: --providers
options.description: A comma delimited list of EventPipe providers to be enabled. This is in the form 'Provider[,Provider]',where Provider is in the form: 'KnownProviderName[:[Flags][:[Level][:[KeyValueArgs]]]]', and KeyValueArgs is in the form: '[key1=value1][;key2=value2]'.  Values in KeyValueArgs that contain ';' or '=' characters need to be surrounded by '"', e.g., FilterAndPayloadSpecs="MyProvider/MyEvent:-Prop1=Prop1;Prop2=Prop2.A.B;".  Depending on your shell, you may need to escape the '"' characters and/or surround the entire provider specification in quotes, e.g., --providers 'KnownProviderName:0x1:1:FilterSpec=\"KnownProviderName/EventName:-Prop1=Prop1;Prop2=Prop2.A.B;\"'. These providers are in addition to any providers implied by the --profile argument. If there is any discrepancy for a particular provider, the configuration here takes precedence over the implicit configuration from the profile.  See documentation for examples.

options.name: --profile
options.description: A named, pre-defined set of provider configurations for common tracing scenarios. You can specify multiple profiles as a comma-separated list. When multiple profiles are specified, the providers and settings are combined (union), and duplicates are ignored.

options.name: --format
options.description: If not using the default NetTrace format, an additional file will be emitted with the specified format under the same output name and with the corresponding format extension. The default format is NetTrace. [default: NetTrace]

options.name: --duration
options.description: When specified, will trace for the given timespan and then automatically stop the trace. Provided in the form of dd:hh:mm:ss.

options.name: --clrevents
options.description: List of CLR runtime events to emit.

options.name: --clreventlevel
options.description: Verbosity of CLR events to be emitted.

options.name: --name
options.description: The name of the process to collect the trace.

options.name: --dport
options.description: The path to a diagnostic port to be used.

options.name: --show-child-io
options.description: Shows the input and output streams of a launched child process in the current console.

options.name: --resume-runtime
options.description: Resume runtime once session has been initialized, defaults to true. Disable resume of runtime using --resume-runtime:false [default: True]

options.name: --stopping-event-provider-name
options.description: A string, parsed as-is, that will stop the trace upon hitting an event with the matching provider name. For a more specific stopping event, additionally provide `--stopping-event-event-name` and/or `--stopping-event-payload-filter`.

options.name: --stopping-event-event-name
options.description: A string, parsed as-is, that will stop the trace upon hitting an event with the matching event name. Requires `--stopping-event-provider-name` to be set. For a more specific stopping event, additionally provide `--stopping-event-payload-filter`.

options.name: --stopping-event-payload-filter
options.description: A string, parsed as [payload_field_name]:[payload_field_value] pairs separated by commas, that will stop the trace upon hitting an event with a matching payload. Requires `--stopping-event-provider-name` and `--stopping-event-event-name` to be set.

options.name: --rundown
options.description: Collect rundown events unless specified false.

options.name: --dsrouter
options.description: The dsrouter command to start. Value should be one of ios, ios-sim, android, android-emu. Run `dotnet-dsrouter -h` for more information.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: collect-linux
commands.description: Collects diagnostic traces using perf_events, a Linux OS technology. collect-linux requires admin privileges to capture kernel- and user-mode events, and by default, captures events from all processes. This Linux-only command includes the same .NET events as dotnet-trace collect, and it uses the kernel's user_events mechanism to emit .NET events as perf events, enabling unification of user-space .NET events with kernel-space system events. Use --probe (optionally with -p|--process-id or -n|--name) to only check which processes can be traced by collect-linux without collecting a trace.

options.name: --providers
options.description: A comma delimited list of EventPipe providers to be enabled. This is in the form 'Provider[,Provider]',where Provider is in the form: 'KnownProviderName[:[Flags][:[Level][:[KeyValueArgs]]]]', and KeyValueArgs is in the form: '[key1=value1][;key2=value2]'.  Values in KeyValueArgs that contain ';' or '=' characters need to be surrounded by '"', e.g., FilterAndPayloadSpecs="MyProvider/MyEvent:-Prop1=Prop1;Prop2=Prop2.A.B;".  Depending on your shell, you may need to escape the '"' characters and/or surround the entire provider specification in quotes, e.g., --providers 'KnownProviderName:0x1:1:FilterSpec=\"KnownProviderName/EventName:-Prop1=Prop1;Prop2=Prop2.A.B;\"'. These providers are in addition to any providers implied by the --profile argument. If there is any discrepancy for a particular provider, the configuration here takes precedence over the implicit configuration from the profile.  See documentation for examples.

options.name: --clreventlevel
options.description: Verbosity of CLR events to be emitted.

options.name: --clrevents
options.description: List of CLR runtime events to emit.

options.name: --perf-events
options.description: Comma-separated list of perf events (e.g. syscalls:sys_enter_execve,sched:sched_switch).

options.name: --probe
options.description: Probe .NET processes for support of the EventPipe UserEvents IPC command used by collect-linux, without collecting a trace. Results list supported processes first. Use '-o stdout' to print CSV (pid,processName,supportsCollectLinux) to the console, or '-o <file>' to write the CSV. Probe a single process with -n|--name or -p|--process-id.

options.name: --profile
options.description: A named, pre-defined set of provider configurations for common tracing scenarios. You can specify multiple profiles as a comma-separated list. When multiple profiles are specified, the providers and settings are combined (union), and duplicates are ignored.

options.name: --output
options.description: The output path for the collected trace data. If not specified it defaults to '<appname>_<yyyyMMdd>_<HHmmss>.nettrace', e.g., 'myapp_20210315_111514.nettrace'. [default: default]

options.name: --duration
options.description: When specified, will trace for the given timespan and then automatically stop the trace. Provided in the form of dd:hh:mm:ss.

options.name: --name
options.description: The name of the process to collect the trace.

options.name: --process-id
options.description: The process id to collect the trace.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Converts traces to alternate formats for use with alternate trace analysis tools. Can only convert from the nettrace format

options.name: --format
options.description: Sets the output format for the trace file conversion.

options.name: --output
options.description: Output filename. Extension of target format will be added.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: input-filename
arguments.description: Input trace file to be converted. Defaults to 'default'. [default: default]

commands.name: list-profiles
commands.description: Lists pre-built tracing profiles with a description of what providers and filters are in each profile

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: ps
commands.description: Lists the dotnet processes that traces can be collected from.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: report
commands.description: Generates a report into stdout from a previously generated trace.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: trace_filename
arguments.description: The file path for the trace being analyzed.

commands.name: report topN
commands.description: Finds the top N methods that have been on the callstack the longest.

options.name: --number
options.description: Gives the top N methods on the callstack. [default: 5]

options.name: --inclusive
options.description: Output the top N methods based on inclusive time. If not specified, exclusive time is used by default.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Output the parameters of each method in full. If not specified, parameters will be truncated.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

arguments.name: trace_filename
arguments.description: The file path for the trace being analyzed.

options.name: --help
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --version
options.description: Show version information
